title: This is the most exciting player in college basketball
body: Big man passers have been a cheat code for years, it's a highly undervalued skill. The NBA mostly forgot about it in between Divac and Jokic. Draymond essentially plays this role as well. I think front offices and coaching have been too focused on dribbling and scoring across the roster. Of course you need those skills, but it's passing that unlocks elite offenses. It's one of the reasons I was so high on Derik Queen and Hansen Yang. Curious if we see more of these guys coming out of college who maybe aren't first scoring options but can be passing hubs
author: @conehed1138

author: @TiffanyBluee

title: PROS and CONS of WASHU in ST. LOUIS
body: washu undergrad here - gonna push back on the mundane thing :) its really possible to leave campus! washU has two metro stops and students get free access to public transpo with a U pass. make any friends with a car here (or get one yourself) and you'll get to explore the magic of St. Louis -- and there is so much to do. amazing botanical gardens, farmers markets, free music and activities - plus washu attempts to transport their students off campus for free for many events. its important to break the washu bubble!!
author: @aprilspringer1075

title: PROS and CONS of WASHU in ST. LOUIS
body: My daughter is a current freshman at WashU.  She has made many good friends.  I visited last November and from what I've seen most students there are hardworking, goal oriented, and friendly.  She told me people do sometimes party, but even those people strive to work hard in classes.  For those that want to go to huge stadiums, skip classes, and (just) party.  Go to a cheaper State school. Don't go to WashU!  If you are looking to get into law, medicine, engineering, or really anything WashU is a good collaborative environment where you will find peers that lift you.  My daughter was valedictorian of her high school and was thrilled just to be around so many brilliant people that inspire her daily.
author: @AK-xu5sj

title: Doctor Reviews SLU-PP-332 Biohack (Is It Worth It?)
body: Since SLU-PP-332 is hydrophobic and has poor bioavailability in oral form, it was given to mice intrapertoneally. Yet, I've seen SLU-PP-332 available only in oral form as a research peptide.  I also discovered that SLU-PP-915 is another E.R.R. pan-agonist that is orally active. Yet, not much seems to be discussed about that.
author: @drmichaellum

title: Doctor Reviews SLU-PP-332 Biohack (Is It Worth It?)
body: I finally found some 100 mg capsules. The 250 mcg capsules are extremely under dosed, especially compared to the lab rat research.

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: Any resident from any city in the US would say that their city sucks. I have lived in Miami for 20 years and this shit is so expensive, if you don't make 6 figures you will live paycheck to paycheck if you are single. I am moving to MO cuz rent in that state is like $800 for a studio in a luxury building. Here in Miami the same studio costs $2000
author: @squalltheonly

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: I've lived in Camdenton Missouri my whole life. Love Lake of the Ozarks. I used to go to marching band competitions in St.Louis at the Edward Jones dome. Always loved going up there.
author: @nickfowler2646

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: you forgot one of the philosophies of the city: that "culture is not just for people with money" - the zoo is free, the muni opera and the symphany all have free seats in the back of the house.
author: @christinehickey9301

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body:
I don't see St Louis as my overall favorite city, but it seemed to offer a good quality of life. Not very glamorous on paper but most can design any life they want.

My biggest negative is the lack of regional growth strategy and concentrated poverty. Biggest pro is you get a lot of big city amenities without the hassle or cost of many other places.
author: @Healthyagingwithmarvin

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: LOVED LOVED LOVED the video! Great job. I miss living in St. Louis; had homes in Shaw as well as on The Hill. Best wishes, Robert at Move to Chattanooga
author: @movetochattanooga

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: I’ve lived in St Louis many years. The summer weather is horrible— very hot. The restaurants and many cultural amenities— such as the symphony, museums and sports are excellent. Traffic problems are very few. I always hear about crime but have NEVER had a problem myself.

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: My mom still stay in fairground neighborhood. I remember the north city was nice back in the 80$. Cheaper that Houston Texas & miss riding the Metrolink that take you all over St Louis area... May be crazy up there but there's no place like home. Al yeah miss Imo's pizza 🍕🍕

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: I just drove across America these past three days and I remember St Louis as a really nice looking city similar to the charm and design of Boston Massachusetts. Was just curious of how living there is.
author: @hackmedia7755

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: Lived there for eight years in the late 80s/early 90s. Overall very enjoyable but so ridiculously hot in the summer. Like “don’t go out” hot. Winter is for sure punctuated by frequent ice storms and miserable cold stretches.
author: @robertsguitarsandgear

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: I am alomst 70 and have always lived here. I also went to UMSL many years ago. Very good education there without the big tuition. I agree with most you said. I worked many years with a very good brokerage called A.G. Edwards now part of Wells Fargo. I would say the winters are much milder than the North. The St. Louis region has really spread out in my lifetime beyond St. Louis county but grew up in the Southhampon neighborhood of the city. It is all abour risk tolerance to crime and watching the 10:00 News is like getting the daily crime report. There are good neighborhoods still in the city but just close to very poor areas riddled with crime (car jacking, stolen cars, broken windows, drive by shooting, teens walking streets with AR-15s) giving them risk as well but like said this is in common with most cities in the U.S.. I only seem to visit the city for Cardinals and Blues games. Soccer is big here thanks to the owners of Enterpsrse and others privately building the new stadium. Many years of Catholic youth leage soccer and now the largest Bosnian immigration in the U.S. help make it so. St. Louis got a bum rap when St. Louis Rams owner Stan Kronke preferred the large TV market of L.A. over a new downtown stadium St. Louis was prepared to build. The subject of Stan Kroenke unifies the people of St. Louis.
author: @larryloveless2967

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]
body: I would recommend anyone who wants to move to Saint Louis to actually move to any of the suburbs west of the city limits. Definitely not any part of Metro East.
author: @Baseball-huh01

title: The REAL Pros and Cons of Living in ST. LOUIS Missouri [HONEST REVIEW]

title: First day of college!  Saint Louis University-Baguio City 🎒 | It’s Ally
body: Batch 2011 here. Best experience ang mag aral sa Baguio city dahil sa freedom. You are on your own and will experience everything that can make the person in you. Magandang environment para maka experience ng independence sa family. On the other hand, madami din napapariwara for the same reason  na sobrang free but nevertheless, dun madalas natututo ang mga student na dumiskarte. Sana naging okay ang experience nyo just like our batch did. It's been fun and nakakamiss mag aral sa baguio. Best days ay pag naglalakad mula main gate to Burnham to SM na nakikipagkwentuhan, spending time kumain sa labas or maglaro sa quantum or magwindow shopping sa department store. Minsan tambay sa internet cafe at drinking with friends naman at night. My last visit to SLU was 2013 and it's never the same kung hindi mo nakkita yung mga classmate mo. Sana naka graduate na lahat ng nasa video and going on with their lives and chosen career. Cheers!
author: @mac2805

title: mini campus tour | washington university in st. louis
body:
~~ Lyrics ~~

footstep noises on stairs
faint piano tune
clothes rustling
chair squeak
charger jostling
card swipe

(hook)
Uh, where do you want to eat then?
We can go to the end of one of the halls - there's a nice area there
Okay, cool - I will show you - yay

(verse 1)
This is the new building
~something~ work to do
doo doo doo we can go on through this door this is brauer.
This is... chemical engineering?

(bridge)
I know!
What? So cool. I did not know this was there
Okay, I really want to do -

(prechorus)
So sorry - I like literally hopped up and down for that
Wait what time is it - what time did it turn on?
Five forty.

(post-chorus)
I'm breaking my back fsvwa[p
The do-not-step-on-seal or you will not graduate
Avoiding that...
Stage where wild is supposed to be [yea]

(interlude)
We may or may not have gotten sushi for dinner.. again [again]
even though we had it for lunch

(verse 2)
Stay alive for the unboxing of our food
Bagels for tomorrow morning,
Some fries just cuz,
and sushi (heehee) because we have problems [and sushi yay]

(elision)
t-okay I can't open this with one hand
[I'll do it]
eughgh this is called one-handed struggles;
[wait we have to open it from the other end ohh okay okay wait]
ta-da

(conclusion)
Oh my god. I'm getting there
[I gotchu]
I did it. [I did it]
and then we have some parmesan fries, just cuz'

yay we're going to eat so bye
author: @ray_as_in_stingray
